recipes.name: Chana Masala
recipes.description: A beloved North Indian classic featuring tender chickpeas in a rich, aromatic tomato-based sauce. Warm spices like cumin, coriander, and garam masala create depth and warmth.
instructions: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add tomato puree and cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add the drained chickpeas and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
Add garam masala and cook for another 2-3 minutes.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with rice or bread.
recipes.tips: For extra creaminess, add a splash of coconut milk or yogurt at the end. You can also use dried chickpeas soaked overnight for a more authentic taste.

recipes.name: Dal Tadka
recipes.description: A simple yet flavorful yellow lentil curry elevated with a fragrant tempering of ghee, cumin seeds, and dried red chilies. Perfect comfort food.
instructions: Rinse the lentils thoroughly under running water until the water runs clear.
In a pressure cooker or pot, add lentils, water, turmeric, and salt.
If using a pressure cooker, cook for 3-4 whistles. If using a pot, simmer for 20-25 minutes until lentils are soft and mushy.
Mash the cooked lentils lightly to achieve a creamy consistency.
In a small pan, heat ghee over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for a few seconds.
Add dried red chilies and sliced garlic, fry until garlic turns golden and chilies darken slightly.
Pour this tempering over the cooked dal and stir well.
Add lemon juice and adjust salt if needed.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with rice or Indian bread.
recipes.tips: For a smoother dal, blend it partially. You can add a splash of milk or cream for extra richness. The tadka (tempering) is key—don't skip it!

recipes.name: Paneer Tikka
recipes.description: Marinated paneer (Indian cottage cheese) cubes grilled to perfection with charred edges. A restaurant-style appetizer that's surprisingly easy to make at home.
instructions: In a bowl, mix yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chilies, and lemon juice.
Add turmeric, red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, salt, and oil. Mix well.
Add paneer cubes and coat evenly with the marinade. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (or up to 4 hours).
Soak wooden skewers in water for 30 minutes to prevent burning.
Thread marinated paneer, bell peppers, and onions alternately onto skewers.
Heat a grill pan or outdoor grill over high heat. Brush with oil.
Grill the skewers for 3-4 minutes on each side until paneer has charred edges and peppers are slightly softened.
Transfer to a serving plate and garnish with fresh cilantro.
Serve hot with mint chutney and lemon wedges.
recipes.tips: Don't over-marinate the paneer as it can become too soft. If grilling outdoors, place paneer and vegetables on separate skewers for better control. You can also bake in an oven at 200°C for 15-20 minutes.

recipes.name: Aloo Gobi
recipes.description: A dry curry of potatoes and cauliflower with aromatic spices. A simple, everyday dish that's packed with flavor and perfect for any meal.
instructions: Heat oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for a few seconds.
Add sliced onions and fry until they turn golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Add minced ginger and green chilies, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the cubed potatoes and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add cauliflower florets and stir well.
Sprinkle turmeric powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix thoroughly.
Cover the pan and cook on medium heat for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until potatoes and cauliflower are tender.
Add garam masala and cook for another minute.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.
recipes.tips: For a crispier texture, increase the heat in the last few minutes. You can also add amchur (dried mango powder) for a tangy flavor. This dish tastes even better the next day!

recipes.name: Baingan Bharta
recipes.description: A smoky, rustic eggplant curry where roasted eggplant is mashed and cooked with aromatic spices. A beloved comfort dish with incredible depth of flavor.
instructions: Roast the eggplants directly over a flame or under the broiler until the skin is charred and the inside is soft, about 10-12 minutes. Alternatively, prick and bake at 200°C for 20 minutes.
Cool the eggplants, peel off the charred skin, and mash the soft flesh.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add chopped onions and fry until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cook for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they soften, about 3-4 minutes.
Add turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add the mashed eggplant and stir to combine evenly.
Cook on medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is well combined and flavors meld.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with rice or bread.
recipes.tips: The charring of the eggplant is crucial for that smoky flavor. Don't skip this step! You can also cook eggplants in a pressure cooker for 1 whistle if you prefer.

recipes.name: Sambar
recipes.description: A tangy, aromatic vegetable stew from South India. Sambar powder gives it a unique, complex flavor that's both spicy and sour.
instructions: Rinse toor dal and cook in a pressure cooker with 2 cups water for 3-4 whistles until soft.
In a large pot, heat oil over medium heat.
Add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add fenugreek seeds, dried red chilies, and curry leaves. Fry for a few seconds.
Add chopped onions and fry until golden brown.
Add chopped vegetables and tomatoes, cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add turmeric powder and sambar powder, stir well.
Add the cooked dal and 2 cups water (or vegetable broth).
Add tamarind paste and salt. Mix well.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Adjust seasoning and serve hot with rice or idli.
recipes.tips: Sambar powder is the key ingredient—use a good quality one or make your own. The balance of spice and sourness is what makes sambar special. You can add more tamarind for extra tanginess.

recipes.name: Palak Paneer
recipes.description: A creamy spinach curry with soft paneer cubes. Rich, nutritious, and absolutely delicious—a restaurant favorite that's easy to make at home.
instructions: Blanch the spinach in boiling water for 2-3 minutes, then drain and blend into a smooth puree.
Heat butter in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add chopped onions and fry until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cook for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the spinach puree and mix well.
Simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add garam masala, salt, and a pinch of nutmeg.
Add the paneer cubes and stir gently to combine.
Pour in the cream and stir well.
Simmer for another 3-4 minutes until paneer is heated through.
Serve hot with rice or Indian bread.
recipes.tips: Don't overcook the spinach before blending—it should retain its vibrant green color. For extra creaminess, use full-fat cream. You can also add a splash of milk if you prefer a lighter version.

recipes.name: Rajma
recipes.description: A hearty kidney bean curry that's comfort food at its finest. Soft beans in a thick, flavorful tomato-based sauce—perfect with rice.
instructions: Drain and rinse the kidney beans.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add chopped onions and fry until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato puree and cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add turmeric, coriander, red chili powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add the kidney beans and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes, allowing flavors to develop.
Add garam masala and cook for another 2-3 minutes.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with rice.
recipes.tips: For a thicker curry, mash some of the beans. For extra flavor, add a bay leaf while simmering. You can also use dried beans soaked overnight for a more authentic taste.

recipes.name: Bhindi Masala
recipes.description: Crispy, dry okra cooked with onions and aromatic spices. A simple, quick dish that's full of flavor and texture.
instructions: Wash and dry the okra thoroughly. Cut into 1-inch pieces and set aside.
Heat oil in a large pan over high heat.
Add sliced onions and fry until they turn golden brown and crispy, about 7-8 minutes.
Add minced ginger and green chilies, cook for 30 seconds.
Add the okra pieces and stir well.
Sprinkle turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Cook on high heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring frequently, until okra is crispy and tender.
Add amchur powder and mix well.
Cook for another minute and then remove from heat.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.
recipes.tips: Make sure the okra is completely dry before cooking to avoid sliminess. Keep the heat high and stir frequently for crispy results. Amchur adds a nice tangy flavor, but it's optional.

recipes.name: Chole Kulche
recipes.description: Spiced chickpeas served with soft, pillowy Indian bread. A street food favorite that's hearty, satisfying, and absolutely delicious.
instructions: For the chole curry: Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds, then onions. Fry until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cook for 1 minute.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add all spices and salt. Mix well.
Add chickpeas and simmer for 10-15 minutes. Garnish with cilantro.
For the kulche: Mix flour, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.
Add yogurt and oil, mix until a soft dough forms.
Knead for 5-7 minutes until smooth. Cover and let rest for 15-20 minutes.
Divide dough into 4 portions and roll into thick discs.
Heat a cast-iron skillet or tawa over high heat.
Place kulche on the hot skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes until puffed and golden.
Flip and cook the other side for another 2-3 minutes.
Serve hot chole with warm kulche.
recipes.tips: The dough should be soft but not sticky. Don't over-knead as it will make the kulche tough. You can brush the cooked kulche with ghee for extra richness.
